task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a tag navigation pane or data tags list showing a tag named 'Reactor_Temp' with an indicator or property showing it is a Float or Numeric type.
Look for the Alarms properties tab for the 'Reactor_Temp' tag. Alarm 1 (or Event 1) should be configured with the type 'Absolute High' and the value/setpoint entered as 180.0.

task_completion.description: Create a user named 'Supervisor' (Username: super) with 'User Right 2' assigned.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Security or User Management section showing a user with the Real Name 'Supervisor' and Username 'super'. There should be a checkbox or access control setting showing 'User Right 2' (or 'Right 2') is granted to this user.

task_completion.description: Configure the 'Reactor_Temp' Alarm 1 to require 'User Right 2' for acknowledgment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Alarms properties tab for the 'Reactor_Temp' tag. The 'Accept', 'Acknowledge', or 'Clear' security requirement field for Alarm 1 must be set to 'User Right 2'.

task_completion.description: Add an 'Alarm Viewer' primitive to Page 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the display/graphics editor showing 'Page 1' with an Alarm Viewer primitive (typically a list or table element designed to show active alarms) placed on the screen canvas.

task_completion.description: Save the project as 'reactor_secure.c3' in the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the application title bar displaying 'reactor_secure.c3' or a 'Save As' dialog box showing the file being saved in 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\CrimsonProjects\' with the name 'reactor_secure.c3'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual HMI configuration software GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the interface of an HMI configuration software (like Crimson 3), with visible menus, toolbars, and dialog boxes being actively used.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor to manually write the project file or spoof the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no text editors (like Notepad or VS Code) open with raw XML or proprietary project file code on the screen.

integrity.description: The project was saved using the software's built-in save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the software's native 'Save' or 'Save As' dialog box being used, rather than terminal commands moving or renaming files.
